checks.a_analysis: The output content is derived directly from the source PDF. No paragraphs, headings, or body text have been fabricated. AI-generated elements like table captions, summaries, and document titles are explicitly allowed and do not constitute fabrication of source content.
The source document contains no contact information such as phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly contains no such information.
The output captures all content across the three pages of the source PDF, including the header title, acronym list, and all objectives listed under Goal 1 and Goal 2.

The tables in the JSON output match the row counts in the source PDF. Table 1 (Goal 1, 1.1-1.3) includes the header and data rows correctly. Table 2 (Goal 1, 1.5-1.8) and Table 3 (Goal 2, 2.1-2.4) also reflect all rows present in the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content within each section and table cell has been preserved. Key descriptions, such as the specific solutions for SGUC under objective 1.1, are fully represented.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output maintains the same meaning as the source. Minor OCR variations in hyphenation or spacing (e.g., 'FIC-' vs 'FIC-') are within acceptable limits and do not alter the information provided.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated content blocks. The repetition of the 'Goal 1' heading at the start of the second table reflects its repetition in the source document at the top of page 2.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document title is correctly identified in the metadata. In the body, major headings are assigned H2, and sub-sections like 'Committee Acronyms' are assigned H3. There are no skips in hierarchy levels.

The complex visual layout of the goals (with sub-header blocks and multi-column rows) has been accurately converted into structured tables. The objective statements (e.g., 1.1, 1.2) are correctly identified as header rows spanning both columns, and the corresponding strategies and leads are placed in correctly aligned data cells.
The reading order in the JSON perfectly follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document across all three pages.

The 'Committee Acronyms' section, while rendered as a paragraph with newlines rather than a formal list object, preserves the structural meaning and distinct lines of the source. There are no other lists to evaluate.
The document contains no interactive links in the source or the output. No links were fabricated or missing.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output significantly alters the visual organization of the objectives and their corresponding data rows. In the source PDF, each numbered objective (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 1.3) is presented as its own distinct block where the objective description serves as a header row directly above its specific content. In the converted HTML, multiple objectives are grouped into single large tables (e.g., 'Goal 1 Objectives and Strategies (1.1 to 1.3)'). Inside these tables, all the objective descriptions are stacked as headers at the very top (rows 1-3), followed by a block of data rows (rows 4-7). This breaks the immediate visual mapping between an objective and its implementation details, as a user must now count rows to associate them correctly. This rearrangement of content flow is a major organizational failure that does not match the source's intent.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Pages 1-3 show objectives (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1) as individual header rows within separate table units, immediately followed by their content.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML combines objectives into groups (1.1-1.3, 1.5-1.8, 2.1-2.4) within single tables, where all objective descriptions are listed sequentially as the first several rows of the table, separated from their respective content rows below.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically in the 'Goal 1 Objectives and Strategies' and 'Goal 2 Objectives and Strategies' tables.

checks.a_analysis: While the logical organization of the tables is flawed (as noted in V1), the technical rendering of the tables is successful. Borders are clearly visible, rows and columns are aligned, and text does not overlap. Headers are visually distinct from body rows via layout. The content remains readable and understandable within the grid structure provided.

The source PDF document consists entirely of text and structured data in tables. There are no images, logos, or charts present in the original material. Consequently, there are no missing or broken visual elements in the HTML output.

There are several instances of duplicated body content in the HTML output. The main document title ('DRAFT' and '2021-2023... Strategic Direction') appears twice at the very beginning of the body content in different styles. Furthermore, the heading 'Goal 1. Improve, expand, and support statewide geospatial data and applications.' appears twice in the main body flow—once before the first set of objectives and again before the second set. While this repetition existed in the PDF due to page breaks (top of page 1 and top of page 2), in a continuous HTML page layout, this constitutes visual duplication of body content. Repetition within the body content itself is a defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Document title 'DRAFT 2021-2023...' appears once as a header. 'Goal 1...' heading appears at the top of Page 1 and Page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML body starts with the title twice (as headings then as plain text). The 'Goal 1...' heading is repeated twice in the body text (once before objectives 1.1-1.3 and once before 1.5-1.8).
checks.b_evidence_location: Start of document and headings before tables.
